            mock_init.assert_called_once_with("example.com", foo="bar")

    def test_closed_signal_call(self):
        class TestSpider(self.spider_class):
            closed_called = False

            def closed(self, reason):
                self.closed_called = True

        crawler = get_crawler()
        spider = TestSpider.from_crawler(crawler, "example.com")
        crawler.signals.send_catch_log(signal=signals.spider_opened, spider=spider)
        crawler.signals.send_catch_log(
            signal=signals.spider_closed, spider=spider, reason=None
        )
        self.assertTrue(spider.closed_called)

    def test_update_settings(self):
        spider_settings = {"TEST1": "spider", "TEST2": "spider"}

        self.spider_class.custom_settings = spider_settings
        settings = Settings(project_settings, priority="project")

        self.spider_class.update_settings(settings)
        self.assertEqual(settings.get("TEST1"), "spider")
        self.assertEqual(settings.get("TEST2"), "spider")
        self.assertEqual(settings.get("TEST3"), "project")

    @inlineCallbacks
    def test_settings_in_from_crawler(self):
        spider_settings = {"TEST1": "spider", "TEST2": "spider"}
        project_settings = {"TEST1": "project", "TEST3": "project"}

        class TestSpider(self.spider_class):
            name = "test"
            custom_settings = spider_settings

            @classmethod
            def from_crawler(cls, crawler: Crawler, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any):
                spider = super().from_crawler(crawler, *args, **kwargs)
                spider.settings.set("TEST1", "spider_instance", priority="spider")
                return spider

        crawler = Crawler(TestSpider, project_settings)
        self.assertEqual(crawler.settings.get("TEST1"), "spider")
        self.assertEqual(crawler.settings.get("TEST2"), "spider")
        self.assertEqual(crawler.settings.get("TEST3"), "project")
        yield crawler.crawl()
        self.assertEqual(crawler.settings.get("TEST1"), "spider_instance")

"""Tests for spider name validation."""
import unittest
from scrapy.spiders import Spider


class SpiderNameValidationTest(unittest.TestCase):
    """Test that spider names are validated properly"""

    def test_valid_spider_name_simple(self):
        """Spider with simple valid name should work"""
        spider = Spider("myspider")
        self.assertEqual(spider.name, "myspider")

    def test_valid_spider_name_with_underscore(self):
        """Spider with underscore in name should work"""
        spider = Spider("my_spider")
        self.assertEqual(spider.name, "my_spider")

    def test_valid_spider_name_with_digits(self):
        """Spider with digits in name should work"""
        spider = Spider("spider123")
        self.assertEqual(spider.name, "spider123")

    def test_valid_spider_name_mixed(self):
        """Spider with mixed case and underscores should work"""
        spider = Spider("MySpider_123")
        self.assertEqual(spider.name, "MySpider_123")

    def test_valid_spider_name_starting_with_underscore(self):
        """Spider with underscore at start should work"""
        spider = Spider("_spider")
        self.assertEqual(spider.name, "_spider")

    def test_invalid_spider_name_with_space(self):
        """Spider with space in name should raise ValueError"""
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm:
            Spider("my spider")
        self.assertIn("invalid", str(cm.exception).lower())

    def test_invalid_spider_name_with_dash(self):
        """Spider with dash in name should raise ValueError"""
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm:
            Spider("my-spider")
        self.assertIn("invalid", str(cm.exception).lower())

    def test_invalid_spider_name_with_dot(self):
        """Spider with dot in name should raise ValueError"""
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm:
            Spider("my.spider")
        self.assertIn("invalid", str(cm.exception).lower())

    def test_invalid_spider_name_with_special_chars(self):
        """Spider with special characters in name should raise ValueError"""
        for name in ["my@spider", "my#spider", "my$spider", "my%spider", "my&spider"]:
            with self.subTest(name=name):
                with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm:
                    Spider(name)
                self.assertIn("invalid", str(cm.exception).lower())

    def test_invalid_spider_name_starting_with_digit(self):
        """Spider starting with digit should raise ValueError"""
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm:
            Spider("123spider")
        self.assertIn("invalid", str(cm.exception).lower())

    def test_invalid_spider_name_empty_string(self):
        """Empty spider name should raise ValueError"""
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm:
            Spider("")
        self.assertIn("invalid", str(cm.exception).lower())

    def test_spider_name_set_via_class_attribute(self):
        """Spider with name set as class attribute should be validated"""
        class MySpider(Spider):
            name = "my_spider"

        spider = MySpider()
        self.assertEqual(spider.name, "my_spider")

    def test_invalid_spider_name_from_class_attribute(self):
        """Spider with invalid class attribute name should raise ValueError"""
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm:
            class MySpider(Spider):
                name = "invalid spider"
            MySpider()
        self.assertIn("invalid", str(cm.exception).lower())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

"""
Base class for Scrapy spiders

See documentation in docs/topics/spiders.rst
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Iterable, List, Optional, Union, cast

from twisted.internet.defer import Deferred

from scrapy import signals
from scrapy.http import Request, Response
from scrapy.utils.trackref import object_ref
from scrapy.utils.url import url_is_from_spider

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    # typing.Self requires Python 3.11
    from typing_extensions import Self

    from scrapy.crawler import Crawler
    from scrapy.settings import BaseSettings
    from scrapy.utils.log import SpiderLoggerAdapter


class Spider(object_ref):
    """Base class for scrapy spiders. All spiders must inherit from this
    class.
    """

    name: str
    custom_settings: Optional[dict] = None

    def __init__(self, name: Optional[str] = None, **kwargs: Any):
        if name is not None:
            self.name = name
        elif not getattr(self, "name", None):
            raise ValueError(f"{type(self).__name__} must have a name")
        
        # Validate spider name
        self._validate_name(self.name)
        
        self.__dict__.update(kwargs)
        if not hasattr(self, "start_urls"):
            self.start_urls: List[str] = []

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_name(name: str) -> None:
        """Validate that spider name is a valid Python identifier."""
        if not isinstance(name, str):
            raise ValueError(
                f"Spider name must be a string, got {type(name).__name__!r}"
            )
        if not name:
            raise ValueError("Spider name cannot be empty")
        if not name.isidentifier():
            raise ValueError(
                f"Invalid spider name {name!r}. Spider names must be valid "
                f"Python identifiers (alphanumeric characters and underscores only, "
                f"and cannot start with a digit)"
            )

    @property
    def logger(self) -> SpiderLoggerAdapter:
        from scrapy.utils.log import SpiderLoggerAdapter

        logger = logging.getLogger(self.name)
        return SpiderLoggerAdapter(logger, {"spider": self})
